---
title: "compute_real_return"
author: "Abidur Rahman"
date: "2026-04-15"
output: html_document
---

```{r setup, include=FALSE}
knitr::opts_chunk$set(echo = TRUE)
```

## R Markdown

This is an R Markdown document. Markdown is a simple formatting syntax for authoring HTML, PDF, and MS Word documents. For more details on using R Markdown see <http://rmarkdown.rstudio.com>.

When you click the **Knit** button a document will be generated that includes both content as well as the output of any embedded R code chunks within the document. You can embed an R code chunk like this:

```{r cars}
summary(cars)
```

## Including Plots

You can also embed plots, for example:

```{r pressure, echo=FALSE}
plot(pressure)
```

Note that the `echo = FALSE` parameter was added to the code chunk to prevent printing of the R code that generated the plot.

### compute_real_return()

**Purpose:**  
Calculates the real return after accounting for inflation.

**What it does:**  
This function converts a nominal return into a real return using the inflation rate. That helps show the growth that actually matters for purchasing power.

**Use case:**  
Use this to compare investment performance across different inflation environments.

**Inputs:**  
- `nominal_return`: Investment return before inflation.
- `inflation_rate`: Inflation rate as a decimal.

**Output:**  
A single numeric value representing the real return.

**Example:**  
`compute_real_return(0.08, 0.03)`
